name: golang-github-zmap-zlint-dev
title: golang-github-zmap-zlint-dev
summary: X.509 Certificate Linter focused on Web PKI standards (library)
description:
X.509 Certificate Linter focused on Web PKI standards (library)
ZLint is a X.509 certificate linter written in Go that checks for
consistency with standards (e.g. RFC 5280
(https://www.ietf.org/rfc/rfc5280.txt)) and other relevant PKI
requirements (e.g. CA/Browser Forum Baseline Requirements
(https://cabforum.org/wp-content/uploads/CA-Browser-Forum-BR-1.4.8.pdf)).
.
This package contains the zlint golang source code library.
use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use golang-github-zmap-zlint-dev because X.509 Certificate Linter focused on Web PKI standards (library).
When the engagement is in the install phase, use golang-github-zmap-zlint-dev as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair golang-github-zmap-zlint-dev with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `golang-github-zmap-zlint-dev --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
